name: sanctum-order
summary: The chapter-sanctum of a holy order — where knights, templars, or sworn initiates gather in council. A round or chapter-house shape, seats for each member, the order's banner and relics at the head.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: round, octagonal, or square — collective, not processional

accent.purpose: the order's sigil inlaid at the center

primary_palette.note: a round table — the grandmaster's seat marked, peers around
a chapter altar at center with seats ringing it — oaths sworn here
the order's founding relic on a plinth, banners and seats arrayed
a grandmaster's throne at one end, tiered benches before it — hierarchical rather than round

story_prompts: a council mid-debate — full chapter seated, voices raised over a map on the central table
between councils — the chamber empty, every seat in place, a single candle burning at the grandmaster's chair
an initiation — a candidate kneeling at the altar, the chapter standing around in silence
a traitor's trial — one seat overturned, the rest occupied, judgment underway
the order has fallen — banners dust-laden, armor-stands holding rusted plate, the table stone cold

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: formal and even (0.4-0.5) — chandelier or ring of candelabra giving even council-light, relic or altar with a stronger highlight

anti_patterns: seating is equal and collective — even a grandmaster's throne is only slightly elevated
the order's sigil, banners, and relics must be present — this room is about identity
no pews in rows — this is a chapter, not a congregation
